name: Biorepository ER with Junction Tables and Many-to-Many
input: Create an ER diagram for a biorepository management system. Entities: Patient (patient_id, mrn, name, dob, sex, consent_status), Sample (sample_id, type, collection_date, volume_ml, storage_location, freeze_thaw_count), Study (study_id, title, pi_name, irb_number, status), Aliquot (aliquot_id, parent_sample_id FK, volume_ml, concentration, created_date), StorageUnit (unit_id, type, temperature, building, room, shelf, position). Relationships: Patient provides Sample (one-to-many). Sample has Aliquot (one-to-many). Study uses Sample (many-to-many — needs junction table StudySample with date_assigned and purpose). Aliquot stored in StorageUnit (many-to-one). Patient enrolled in Study (many-to-many — needs junction table PatientStudy with enrollment_date and consent_form_id). Show all PKs, FKs, and cardinalities. At least 7 entities including junction tables.
qualityCriteria: All 5 core entities present (Patient, Sample, Study, Aliquot, StorageUnit)
Junction table StudySample or equivalent present
Junction table PatientStudy or equivalent present
At least 3 attributes per major entity
Valid Mermaid syntax that parses without errors
napkinBenchmark: ER diagram with 7 entities (5 core + 2 junction), many-to-many via junction tables, full PKs/FKs, cardinalities.
